node.title: Food Delivery app -- LLD -- Need Reviews

topic.title: Food Delivery app -- LLD -- Need Reviews

post.content:
Hey there, 

I think the data modelling looks decent, but I would suggest you to create some classes which acts as model repositories similar to coupon manager (like OrderService, UserService etc). I will always try to use either a Service or Repository class to do this. 

Also, It's better to have abstraction and allow your driver code to have access functions which are only specified in the requirements, i.e. If the requirement says "The user should be able to order" then create a function like "orderFood". I will use a Controller to do this job (This holds the core business logic)

You can use a strategy pattern in this case to choose an algorithm at runtime (Like chooseCoupon or chooseDriver etc) and your classes should not have any tight couplings and you should always inject dependencies. 

Finally, Use your driver class to create different objects and do dependency injections. 

Have a great day :)
you can add a singleton class for your system and also a database interface to store data.
PaymentObject can\'t be a class name , use something like PaymentDetail
you can add singleton and remove company tags. no spamming!
